gaps.note: No worklogs/ at repo root; required by Phenotype cross-project governance. session docs under docs/sessions/ are session-style, not worklog format.

gaps.item: deploy markers (Dockerfile, helm, k8s, vercel, netlify, render, fly)
gaps.note: Pure Python package; docs/deployment.md states 'no Docker or Kubernetes manifests'. Acceptable for CLI library but blocks container scoring.
No .github/release-drafter.yml; changelog exists but release notes are manual.
No HTTP service; only internal 'healthy' boolean in src/helios_bench/__init__.py for leak detection. N/A for CLI but flagged for any future service.
None present; no service API surface in this CLI harness.
No screenshots, demo gif, or tutorial link; UX richness is low (8 sections, text-only).
No root ADR docs; decisions scattered in docs/sessions/. Phenotype spec system expects root-level ARCHITECTURE.
No top-level spec.md; only sub-specs under docs/operations/ and docs/sessions/.

strengths: Full Phenotype Org compliance (CODEOWNERS, SECURITY, COC, FUNDING, LICENSE, PR/ISSUE templates, CHANGELOG, CITATION.cff)
Conventional Commits + branch discipline documented in CONTRIBUTING.md
Journey-gate workflow wired to phenotype-tooling reusable workflow
Local quality gates via Taskfile (build, test, lint, clean)
VitePress docsite with sessions, operations, journeys subdirs
Pre-commit, SonarCloud, TruffleHog, Dependabot, scorecard all in place
